summary: Joined Wescom Credit Union in March 2006 after a panel interview at their Pasadena office, where I opened by explaining that every letter of my first name represents a member of my family — a personal story the panel appreciated, delivered with the same calm confidence I brought to the job itself. As a Call Center Representative, I assisted members with checking and savings accounts and prepared them for handoff to loan officers for more specialized needs. One call has always stuck with me: an extremely frustrated, profane member who, by the end of the conversation, apologized and thanked me for staying patient after I stayed calm, listened, and connected them with a loan officer who improved their rate. That call reinforced a lesson that has stayed with me ever since — people are usually frustrated with their situation, not with the person trying to help them. Staying composed and non-defensive under pressure became one of my strongest professional traits, one I still rely on supporting users during outages and communicating with executives in high-pressure moments. I left in July 2007 to pursue a role at Bank of America's headquarters, right next door.
highlights: Landed the role via a panel interview at Wescom's Pasadena office, opening with a personal story about how each letter of my name represents a family member — calm, confident communication that set the tone for the role.
Assisted members with checking and savings accounts and prepared them for handoff to loan officers for more specialized financial needs.

De-escalated a call with an extremely frustrated, profane member by staying calm and focused on solving their problem — connected them with a loan officer who improved their rate, and the member apologized and thanked me by the end of the call.
Reinforced a lesson that has stuck for the rest of my career: people are usually frustrated with their situation, not with the person trying to help them.
Spent roughly a year and a half in the role before leaving to pursue a position at Bank of America's headquarters, located next door in Pasadena.
